protocol: Seventh Law clause 5 — Recurring Bluff Hunt

targets.mutation_applied: Replaced URL path '/comment/' with '/comments/' (wrong path) in RuTorComments.getComments — both pageless and paged branches.
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: RuTorCommentsTest > getComments fetches the canonical comment URL... org.junit.ComparisonFailure at RuTorCommentsTest.kt:47; RuTorCommentsTest > getComments with page greater than zero appends ?page=... ComparisonFailure at RuTorCommentsTest.kt:72
targets.remediation: N/A — test correctly asserts on the URL the user's tap triggers (server.takeRequest().path). Falsifiable, primary assertion is on a network surface.

targets.production_class_under_test: lava.tracker.rutracker.mapper.RuTrackerDtoMappers (the reverse mapper used by SwitchingNetworkApi on the SDK path)
targets.mutation_applied: In RuTrackerDtoMappers.loginResultToDto, swapped the AuthState.Authenticated success branch to always return AuthResponseDto.WrongCredits(captcha=null) — even with a valid sessionToken.
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: SwitchingNetworkApiAuthTest > login routes via SDK on direct rutracker and reverse mapper produces user-visible AuthResponseDto FAILED at line 166; SwitchingNetworkApiAuthTest > checkAuthorized routes via SDK and returns true when SDK reports Authenticated FAILED at line 114 (the assertion on AuthResponseDto.Success failed).
targets.remediation: N/A — test asserts on user-visible token and user.id/avatarUrl values produced by the reverse mapper round-trip; the legacy api fall-through is also asserted via .calls.isEmpty(). Real LavaTrackerSdk + DefaultTrackerRegistry + FakeTrackerClient build the full graph.

targets.mutation_applied: Added a 14th enum value MUTATION_EXTRA_VALUE_FOR_BLUFF_HUNT after USER_PROFILE.
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: TrackerCapabilityTest > enum has 13 values matching spec FAILED at line 9; TrackerCapabilityTest > contains all named capabilities FAILED at line 15.
targets.remediation: N/A — test pins the enum cardinality + every name. Any addition or rename breaks it loudly.

targets.production_class_under_test: lava.data.impl.service.connectTarget (extension on Endpoint, used by ConnectionServiceImpl.isReachable)
targets.mutation_applied: Changed `is Endpoint.GoApi -> host to port` to `is Endpoint.GoApi -> host to HTTPS_PORT` (drop the user-supplied port, hard-code 443).
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: ConnectionTargetTest > GoApi probes declared port FAILED at line 37 (8443 != 443); ConnectionTargetTest > GoApi probes non-default port FAILED at line 48 (9443 != 443).
targets.remediation: N/A — test exercises the same connectTarget extension production isReachable() calls; primary assertion is on the (host,port) pair the TCP probe opens — exactly what the user observes via the green-icon indicator.

targets.mutation_applied: In EndpointConverter.toJson, dropped the put(PortKey, port) call for Endpoint.GoApi.
targets.test_failure_message_if_failed: EndpointConverterTest > convert GoApi to json includes host and port FAILED at line 78 (org.json.JSONException — port not present); EndpointConverterTest > convert GoApi with non-default port to json preserves port FAILED at line 87; EndpointConverterTest > roundtrip GoApi survives toJson then fromJson with port preserved FAILED at line 123 (default port 8443 instead of 9443 after round-trip).
targets.remediation: N/A — primary assertion on persisted JSON content + the rehydrated Endpoint.GoApi.port field. Round-trip test specifically guards user-visible 'next launch reaches the right port' behavior.

summary.verdict: All 5 randomly-sampled tests survived the mutation-and-revert protocol. Each test failed cleanly when its production class was deliberately broken and passed again after revert. No bluff fakes detected at this phase boundary.
